name: LangGraph checkpointing and thread persistence
context_primitives: LangGraph provides durable checkpoints for graph state (variables, pending tool calls, branch metadata) keyed by thread_id, separate from raw LLM context. Developers choose checkpointer backends (memory, SQLite, Postgres) to resume workflows and implement human-in-the-loop interrupts.
deployment_pattern: Configure checkpointer in graph compile; persist thread IDs in your app DB; combine with external vector store for RAG if needed.
benchmarks_eval_alignment: Framework capability rather than benchmark; quality depends on what you serialize into state vs. what you re-fetch.
ecosystem_integrations: LangChain ecosystem, LangSmith tracing, common SQL/vector stores.
differentiators: Application **control-plane** memory (orchestration state) vs. semantic user memory products—often used together.
significance_for_builders: Use LangGraph checkpoints for **reliable multi-step execution**, not as a substitute for curated semantic memory unless you deliberately store embeddings in state.
key_timeline_or_milestones: LangGraph persistence docs maintained alongside LangGraph 0.x/1.x releases [exact version N/A].
